symptoms.symptom_name: Bilateral pressing non-throbbing band-like headache, not activity-aggravated, no nausea

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral pressing non-throbbing pain without nausea/activity aggravation meets tension-type criteria.
These features argue against migraine (unilateral, throbbing, nausea, activity-aggravated).

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Subarachnoid haemorrhage / other serious secondary headache
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of thunderclap onset, meningism, focal deficits and GCA features argues strongly against a serious secondary headache.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pericranial muscle tenderness; no analgesic overuse

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pericranial muscle tenderness supports tension-type headache but is elicited on examination.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: No imaging/bloods for primary tension-type headache without red flags.
Only if red flags (thunderclap/meningism → CT/LP; suspected GCA → ESR/CRP) — via ED.
